steps.title: Resolve the timeline problem
steps.content: The artifacts may date from the thirteenth century, but the settlement may have been founded in the fourteenth century. If both claims are true, the artifacts must be older than the settlement itself.

steps.title: Explain how older items arrived
steps.content: Choice A compares artifact frequency, Choice C speculates about the founders' region, and Choice D discusses excavation damage; none resolves the timing conflict. Older artifacts at a later-founded site would need to have come from elsewhere. The answer is B.
